headline: Zebra Stock Soars on Earnings. The Automation Business Is Turning.
summary: Zebra reported earnings per share of $4.75, up 18.2% year over year, from sales of $1.5 billion, up 14.3% year over year. Wall Street was looking for $4.25 and $1.5 billion, respectively.

content.title: Zebra Stock Soars on Earnings. The Automation Business Is Turning.
content.summary: Zebra reported earnings per share of $4.75, up 18.2% year over year, from sales of $1.5 billion, up 14.3% year over year. Wall Street was looking for $4.25 and $1.5 billion, respectively.

headline: Zebra Technologies lifts annual sales forecast on strong automation demand
summary: Zebra Technologies on Tuesday raised its annual sales growth forecast, betting on ‌robust demand for its products that help ‌automate manufacturing workflows, sending its shares 15% higher in premarket ​trading. The company has benefited as warehouses, retailers and logistics firms increase automation to manage supply chains and monitor operations more closely. Zebra's products include enterprise software ‌and hardware such ⁠as barcode scanners, RFID readers, industrial printers and autonomous robots.

headline: Zebra’s (NASDAQ:ZBRA) Q1 CY2026: Beats On Revenue, Stock Jumps 14.2%
summary: Enterprise data capture company Zebra Technologies (NASDAQ:ZBRA) reported Q1 CY2026 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 14.3% year on year to $1.50 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $4.75 per share was 11.9% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Interactive Kiosk Market Analysis Report 2026: Rising Demand Across Airports, Retail, Entertainment, and BFSI, & Escalating Preference for Contactless Payments - Global Forecast to 2030
summary: Interactive kiosks, pivotal in industries like retail, healthcare, and travel, are experiencing robust market growth. Forecasted to expand from $34.26 billion in 2024 to $54.51 billion by 2030, this growth is driven by self-service technology adoption, digital solutions for customer service, and innovations like AI and wireless connectivity. headline: Zebra (ZBRA) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: William Burns: Thank you, Mike.  Today, I want to take a step back to put our results into a broader context to what our performance says about Zebra's position in the market, the momentum in our business and the opportunity ahead.  First, we achieved strong Q1 results, and we are seeing continued momentum that supports our increased outlook for the full year.

content.title: Zebra (ZBRA) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript

Today, I want to take a step back to put our results into a broader context to what our performance says about Zebra's position in the market, the momentum in our business and the opportunity ahead.  First, we achieved strong Q1 results, and we are seeing continued momentum that supports our increased outlook for the full year.

headline: Zebra Technologies lifts annual sales forecast on strong automation demand (May 12)
summary: Zebra ‌Technologies on Tuesday raised its annual sales ‌growth forecast, betting on robust demand for its products that help ​automate manufacturing workflows, sending its shares 15% higher in early trading. The company has benefited as warehouses, retailers and logistics firms increase automation to manage supply chains and ‌monitor operations more ⁠closely. Zebra's products include enterprise software and hardware such as barcode scanners, RFID readers ⁠and industrial printers.

The company has benefited as warehouses, retailers and logistics firms increase automation to manage supply chains and ‌monitor operations more ⁠closely. Zebra's products include enterprise software and hardware such as barcode scanners, RFID readers ⁠and industrial printers.
